title: Unidentified Sith sorceress
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Character |type=Sith |image= |name=Unidentified Sith sorceress |homeworld=[[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]] {{C|adopted}}''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'' |birth=Prior to 6200 BBY |death=Before [[6000 BBY/Legends|6000 BBY]] {{C|Ambria}}''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]''According to ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'', the dark side energies of the sorceress were already present at the end of the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] (dated [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]] per ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''). ''Tales of the Jedi Companion'' also states that the character died millennia prior to 4000 BBY, placing her death at 6000 BBY at the latest, and as it states she toiled over the construction of her obelisk for centuries, she must have been born sometime prior to 6200 BBY. |species= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Sith/Legends|Sith]]''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Oss Wilum told me you chose this world because the dark side is strong here."
"Yes. There was a great darkness here.|Nomi Sunrider and Thon, regarding the legacy of the Sith sorceress on Ambria|[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]]}} A '''Sith sorceress''' inhabited the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]] [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] before the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. As a [[Darksider|practitioner]] of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and a follower of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith Order]], [[Gender/Legends|she]] spent hundreds of [[Standard year/Legends|years]] [[Slavery/Legends|enslaving]] Ambria's [[Species/Legends|populace]], overseeing the construction of a large [[Ambria Sith obelisk|obelisk]]. Upon completing her creation, she attempted to bind the planet to her will by means of a Sith [[Force power/Legends|ritual]]. The sorceress, however, was unable to control the dark side power she had invoked, unleashing a wave of [[The Force/Legends|Force]] energy that [[Devastation of Ambria|devastated]] Ambria and [[Death/Legends|ended]] her life. ==Biography== ===Subjugation of Ambria=== [[File:Ambria_FF11.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Ambria, the domain of the Sith sorceress.]] A [[Gender/Legends|female]] disciple of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith Order]], the [[Sith sorcerer|Sith sorceress]] lived [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] before the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. In her time, she established a domain on [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]], a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]. Under the influence of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], she [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] Ambria's [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|inhabitants]] and exploited the native fauna as beasts of burden. Through her efforts, the sorceress depleted much of Ambria's natural resources, endeavoring to create a [[Ambria Sith obelisk|large obelisk]] in the north central regions of the planet. Determined to finish her creation, she oversaw the construction of the [[black]]-[[Color/Legends|colored]] spike-shaped [[obelisk]] for hundreds of [[Standard year/Legends|years]], infusing it with the power of the dark side.{{CSWECite|I|26|Ambria}} After finally completing her obelisk, the sorceress conducted a complicated Sith [[Force power/Legends|ritual]], attempting to bind the entire world of Ambria to her will. In the process, she invoked a level of dark side power that ultimately overshadowed her own abilities. As a result, she lost control of the wave of [[The Force/Legends|Force]] energy that was subsequently unleashed upon Ambria. The failed machinations of the sorceress not only [[Devastation of Ambria|devastated]] Ambria's surface, but also [[Death/Legends|killed]] every living being on the planet, including herself. ===Legacy=== {{Quote|It… it's a ''wasteland''.|Nomi Sunrider on Ambria|}} Due to the botched ritual of the fallen Sith sorceress, the power of the dark side continued to plague the planet of Ambria for centuries. Following the end of the Great Hyperspace War in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' survivors from the defeated [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] would eventually take refuge on Ambria, hiding among the dark side energies caused by the sorceress. By the time of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], the name of the sorceress had been forgotten, though her deeds were nonetheless recorded among [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] lore. The remnants of her existence were encountered by [[Thon]], a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who trapped Ambria's dark side energies inside the large [[Lake|body]] of [[Water/Legends|water]] known as [[Lake Natth]]. The exploits of the sorceress were not completely undone, however, as the planet remained a barren wasteland for several further [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]].''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' ==Personality and traits== The Sith sorceress was a female follower of the dark side-worshipping Sith Order. While inhabiting the world of Ambria, the sorceress—driven insane by the dark side—subjugated both the sentient and non-sentient population, aiming to dominate the whole planet under her influence. Living for centuries up to the culmination of her work, she obsessively pursued the creation of the Sith obelisk. ==Powers and abilities== A powerful [[Darksider|practitioner]] of the dark side of the Force, the Sith sorceress created a large obelisk on Ambria, imbuing the structure with dark side energies. Additionally, she possessed the knowledge of a Sith ritual which, if completed, would have allowed her to bind an entire planet to her will. By conducting this ritual, she called upon a magnitude of dark side power surpassing the efforts of any individual before her time. The sorceress lacked the capacity to control this power, however, and was unable to prevent it from ending her life and devastating Ambria. ==Behind the scenes== The Sith sorceress of Ambria was introduced in ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'', a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] supplement published by [[West End Games]] in [[1996]]. While the character's name and species remained unidentified, her activities were later mentioned in [[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction|''Path of Destruction'']] and [[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil|''Dynasty of Evil'']], two novels from the [[Star Wars: Darth Bane Trilogy|''Darth Bane Trilogy'']]. ==Appearances== *''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' {{Ab|Darth Bane: Path of Destruction (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]'' {{Ab|Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil (audiobook)}} {{Vision|[[Darth Cognus]]}} ==Sources== *''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'' *{{CSWECite|I|26|Ambria}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Ambrians]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified species]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]] [[Category:Sith sorcerers]]
